briefdescription.para: Advance a ring buffer index, wrapping at capacity.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer to the ring buffer control struct.
Pointer to the caller-provided backing array.
Size of the backing array in bytes. Must be > 0. One byte is used as a sentinel, so usable capacity is size - 1.

briefdescription.para: Reset the ring buffer to empty.
Put a byte into the ring buffer.

simplesect.para: true if the byte was stored, false if the buffer is full.

briefdescription.para: Get a byte from the ring buffer.

simplesect.para: true if a byte was available, false if the buffer is empty.

briefdescription.para: Peek at the next byte without removing it.

simplesect.para: true if a byte was available, false if the buffer is empty.

briefdescription.para: Check if the ring buffer is full.
Check if the ring buffer is empty.
Return the number of bytes currently stored in the buffer.
Return the number of free bytes available for writing.
Write multiple bytes into the ring buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Writes up to len bytes from data. Returns the number of bytes actually written (may be less than len if the buffer fills up). Uses memcpy internally for efficiency across wrap boundaries.

briefdescription.para: Read multiple bytes from the ring buffer.

detaileddescription.para: Reads up to len bytes into data. Returns the number of bytes actually read (may be less than len if the buffer doesn't have enough). Uses memcpy internally for efficiency across wrap boundaries.

parameterdescription.para: Maximum number of bytes to read.

briefdescription.para: Peek at up to len bytes without removing them.

detaileddescription.para: Copies bytes starting from the read position into data without advancing the tail. Subsequent calls to syn_ringbuf_read will return the same bytes.

parameterdescription.para: Maximum number of bytes to peek.

simplesect.para: Number of bytes actually copied (may be less than len).
